doc.id: Potkay and Feng - 2023 - Do stomata optimize turgor-driven growth A new framework for integrating stomata response with whol_appendix.pdf
doc.title: Do stomata optimize turgor-driven growth? (Supporting Information: Notes S1 – Full Model, Parameter Estimation, and Simulation description)

model_card.name: S3 Leaf temperature and stomatal conductances
task: compute leaf temperature TL from energy balance
compute transpiration E from diffusion conductances
derive dE/dgw and mapping between gw and gc for downstream λ=dAn/dE

assumptions.text: Leaf temperature TL is obtained numerically as the root of the leaf energy balance.

evidence.snippet: Leaf temperature is calculated numerically as the value that satisfies the energy balance ... I = εσTL^4 + LE + Cp gb,H(TL − Ta), (S3.1)

assumptions.text: Boundary-layer conductance to heat transfer gb,H is approximated by boundary-layer conductance to vapor transfer gb (difference only by a factor 1.08 under laminar flow with stomata on both sides).

evidence.snippet: gb,H ... we approximate by ... gb, since the heat and vapor conductances differ only by a factor of 1.08 under laminar flow for leaves with stomata on both sides

assumptions.text: Absorbed solar insolation I is computed via a Beer–Lambert canopy formulation; φ is treated constant and LAI relates to total leaf area aL via canopy width W.

evidence.snippet: I = Is cos(Φ)/(φ LAI) [1 − exp(−κL LAI sec(Φ))], (S3.2)
φ ... treated as a constant ... LAI ... related to ... aL ... by canopy width W ... (LAI = aL·φ−1·W−2)

assumptions.text: Transpiration E can be expressed using diffusion conductances gw and gb and the air-to-leaf vapor pressure gradient (DL or e′L(TL)−ea).

evidence.snippet: E = DL / (Patm(1/gw + 1/gb)) = (e′L(TL) − ea(Ta,RH)) / (Patm(1/gw + 1/gb)), (S3.3)

assumptions.text: Total conductance to CO2 transfer gc is derived from gw and gb using fixed coefficients (1.6 and 1.37).

equations.explanation: Leaf energy balance used to solve leaf temperature.

evidence.snippet: I=εσTL^4 + LE + Cp gb,H(TL − Ta), (S3.1)

equations.explanation: Beer–Lambert canopy formulation for absorbed solar insolation (leaf-area averaged).

evidence.snippet: I = Is cos(Φ)/(φ LAI) [1 − exp(−κL LAI sec(Φ))], (S3.2)

equations.explanation: Relationship between leaf area index and total leaf area via canopy width.

evidence.snippet: LAI ... related to total leaf area, aL, by ... W ... (LAI = aL·φ-1·W-2)

equations.explanation: Transpiration from vapor-pressure gradient and series conductances (stomatal+boundary layer).

evidence.snippet: E = DL/(Patm(1/gw + 1/gb)) = (e′L(TL) − ea(Ta,RH))/(Patm(1/gw + 1/gb)), (S3.3)

equations.explanation: Sensitivity of leaf temperature to stomatal water-vapor conductance from differentiated energy balance.

evidence.snippet: dTL/dgw = − L (dE/dgw) / (4εσTL^3 + Cp gb). (S3.4)

equations.explanation: Total derivative of E w.r.t. gw accounting for TL-dependence of saturation vapor pressure.

evidence.snippet: dE/dgw = ∂E/∂gw + ∂E/∂e′L · (de′L/dTL) · (dTL/dgw), (S3.5)
∂E/∂gw = DL / (Patm(1+gw/gb)^2) (S3.6)

equations.explanation: Closed-form expression for dE/dgw used downstream to compute λ.

evidence.snippet: dE/dgw = E/gw^2 · 1/(1/gw + 1/gb) + (sL/Patm)/(4εσTL^3 + Cp gb), (S3.8)

equations.explanation: Transform between water-vapor conductances and total CO2 conductance.
Derivative needed downstream (Section S4) when λ uses gc.

variables.name: leaf temperature
variables.definition: Leaf temperature solved from the energy balance (Eq. S3.1).

evidence.snippet: Leaf temperature, TL [K] ... calculated numerically ... energy balance ... (S3.1)

variables.name: air temperature
variables.definition: Air temperature in the energy balance and vapor-pressure terms.

evidence.snippet: Ta is the air temperature [K]

variables.name: leaf area-averaged absorbed solar insolation
variables.definition: Absorbed solar radiation term used in the leaf energy balance.

evidence.snippet: I is the leaf area-averaged absorbed solar insolation [W∙m-2]

variables.name: incoming insolation
variables.definition: Incoming shortwave radiation used in Eq. S3.2.

evidence.snippet: Is is the incoming insolation [W∙m-2]

variables.name: solar elevation below the zenith
variables.definition: Angular term used in Eq. S3.2 (cos, sec).

evidence.snippet: Φ is the solar elevation below the zenith [-]

variables.name: canopy extinction coefficient
variables.definition: Dimensionless extinction coefficient in Beer–Lambert canopy light attenuation.

evidence.snippet: κL is a dimensionless canopy extinction coefficient [-]

variables.name: ratio of total- to projected-leaf area
variables.definition: Used in Eq. S3.2 and the LAI–aL relation; treated constant here.

evidence.snippet: φ is the ratio of total- to projected-leaf area [-] which is here treated as a constant

variables.name: leaf area index
variables.definition: Leaf area index used in Eq. S3.2.

evidence.snippet: LAI is the leaf area index [-]

variables.name: total leaf area
variables.definition: Total leaf area used to derive LAI.

evidence.snippet: LAI is related to total leaf area, aL, by the canopy width, W ... (LAI = aL·φ-1·W-2)

variables.name: canopy width
variables.definition: Canopy width used in LAI = aL·φ−1·W−2.

evidence.snippet: LAI ... related to ... aL, by the canopy width, W [m] (LAI = aL·φ-1·W-2)

variables.name: transpiration rate
variables.definition: Transpiration expressed via vapor pressure gradient and conductances (Eq. S3.3).

evidence.snippet: transpiration ... E ... defined by ... conductance to water vapor ... gw ... (S3.3)

variables.name: diffusional conductance to water vapor from the leaf
variables.definition: Leaf conductance to water vapor (stomatal-side term used in Eq. S3.3).

evidence.snippet: diffusional conductance to water vapor from the leaf, gw [mol∙m-2∙s-1]

variables.name: boundary-layer conductance to vapor transfer
variables.definition: Boundary-layer conductance to vapor transfer (series with gw in Eq. S3.3).

evidence.snippet: gb,H ... we approximate by ... gb ... boundary layer conductance to vapor transfer (gb)

variables.name: boundary-layer conductance to heat transfer
variables.definition: Approximated by gb (see A2) for laminar flow leaves with stomata on both sides.

evidence.snippet: gb,H is the boundary layer conductance to heat transfer [mol∙m-2∙s-1]

variables.name: air-to-leaf vapor pressure deficit
variables.definition: Vapor pressure deficit used in Eq. S3.3.

evidence.snippet: DL is the air-to-leaf vapor pressure deficit [kPa]

variables.name: atmospheric pressure
variables.definition: Atmospheric pressure used in Eq. S3.3 and derivatives.

evidence.snippet: Patm is the atmospheric pressure [kPa]

variables.name: saturation vapor pressure at leaf temperature
variables.definition: Saturation vapor pressure evaluated at TL.

evidence.snippet: e’L is the saturation vapor pressure of the leaf at TL [kPa]

variables.name: air vapor pressure at air temperature and RH
variables.definition: Air vapor pressure evaluated for RH and Ta.

evidence.snippet: ea is the vapor pressure of the air evaluated for the RH and Ta [kPa]

variables.name: relative humidity
variables.definition: Relative humidity used to compute ea(Ta,RH) in Eq. S3.3.

evidence.snippet: ea is the vapor pressure of the air evaluated for the RH and Ta [kPa]

variables.name: slope of saturation vapor pressure response to temperature at TL
variables.definition: Shorthand for de′L/dTL in Eq. S3.8.

evidence.snippet: s is the slope ... shorthand for de’L/dTL [kPa∙K-1]

variables.name: total conductance to CO2 transfer
variables.definition: Derived from gw and gb (Eq. S3.9).

evidence.snippet: Once gw is known, we calculate the total conductance to CO2 transfer, gc [mol CO2∙m-2∙s-1]
ε is the emissivity [-]
σ is the Stefan–Boltzmann constant [W∙m-2∙K-4]
L is the latent heat of vaporization [J∙(mol H2O)-1]

parameters.name: C_p
parameters.estimation: Approximated by theoretical specific heat capacity of dry air.

evidence.snippet: Cp is the ... specific heat capacity ... which we approximate by the theoretical specific heat capacity of dry air
κL is a dimensionless canopy extinction coefficient [-]

parameters.name: phi
parameters.estimation: Treated as a constant in this section.

evidence.snippet: φ ... treated as a constant

steps: Compute LAI from aL, φ, and W (LAI = aL·φ−1·W−2).
Compute absorbed insolation I using Eq. S3.2.
Solve for leaf temperature TL numerically such that the energy balance Eq. S3.1 holds.
Compute transpiration E using Eq. S3.3.
Compute dE/dgw using Eqs. S3.4–S3.8 (including sL = de′L/dTL).
Compute gc from gw and gb using Eq. S3.9 and compute ∂gw/∂gc using Eq. S3.10.

evidence.snippet: Leaf temperature is calculated numerically as the value that satisfies the energy balance ... (S3.1)
Substituting ... we have a closed solution for dE/dgw required in the Section S4 ... (S3.8)

limitations.text: The numerical method used to solve TL from the energy balance is not specified in this section.

evidence.snippet: Leaf temperature is calculated numerically as the value that satisfies the energy balance

limitations.text: gb,H is approximated by gb (heat vs vapor conductances differ by a factor 1.08), which may not hold outside the stated flow/leaf assumptions.

evidence.snippet: we approximate ... gb ... differ only by a factor of 1.08 under laminar flow for leaves with stomata on both sides

implementation.model_scope: Instantaneous leaf energy balance and conductance transformations used to compute TL, E, and derivatives for stomatal optimization and photosynthesis coupling.

evidence.snippet: Leaf temperature ... satisfies the energy balance ... (S3.1)
E ... defined by ... gw ... (S3.3)
Leaf temperature is calculated numerically as the value that satisfies the energy balance

unresolved_assets.label: gb (boundary-layer conductance) formulation
unresolved_assets.issue: Section S3 uses gb in E and gc transforms but does not specify how gb is calculated (e.g., from wind speed/leaf dimension).

unresolved_assets.label: e′(T) and slope sL = de′/dT
unresolved_assets.issue: Section S3 defines sL as de′L/dTL and uses e′L(TL), but does not provide explicit functional forms in this section.

evidence.snippet: s ... shorthand for de’L/dTL [kPa∙K-1]

open_questions.question: What exact formula(s) are used to compute boundary-layer conductance gb (and gb,H if not approximated), including required inputs (wind speed, leaf width, etc.)?
open_questions.why_needed: gb is required for E (Eq. S3.3), dE/dgw (Eq. S3.8), and gc transform (Eq. S3.9–S3.10).
open_questions.likely_location: Elsewhere in SI or main text (methods/parameterization) or referenced sources (e.g., Campbell & Norman).

open_questions.question: What functional forms are used for saturation vapor pressure e′(T) and for ea(Ta,RH), and how is DL defined numerically (units/temperature conventions)?
open_questions.why_needed: E depends on e′L(TL) − ea(Ta,RH) (Eq. S3.3) and dE/dgw uses sL = de′L/dTL (Eq. S3.8).
open_questions.likely_location: Referenced psychrometric relations or a dedicated methods/appendix section.

evidence.snippet: e’L ... saturation vapor pressure ... ea ... vapor pressure ... (S3.3)
s ... shorthand for de’L/dTL [kPa∙K-1]

open_questions.question: Which numerical solver/root-finding method is used to solve TL from Eq. S3.1 and what convergence criteria are applied?
open_questions.why_needed: Implementation requires a stable and reproducible TL solution to feed photosynthetic temperature dependencies in S4.
open_questions.likely_location: Implementation details in main text methods or code supplement (if any).

evidence.snippet: Leaf temperature is calculated numerically as the value that satisfies the energy balance

section_titles: S3 Leaf temperature and stomatal conductances
chunk_plan.rationale: Defines TL via energy balance, transpiration via diffusion conductances, and derivatives/transforms needed to couple stomata control with photosynthesis in S4.
